display.purpose: Headline metrics only (fleet $/hr, headcount, blast counts). Geist Mono, tabular. The only large type in the system.

headline.purpose: Tracked uppercase section markers (RETICLE // SECTOR). Not big — tracked.

title.purpose: Agent names, incident titles, panel headers.

label.purpose: Data labels, units, column heads. Mono uppercase tracked, muted.

shadows.name: crit-state-lift
shadows.purpose: The single shadow in the system: a critical card lifts (translateY -4px) to win the visual fight — a response to state, never ambient.

motion.name: ret-heartbeat
motion.purpose: A status dot that breathes on a degrading agent and races on a critical one. Health-paced, not decorative.

motion.name: ret-sonar
motion.purpose: Critical agents ping outward as peripheral bait — the only thing that draws the eye across the board.

motion.name: ret-promote-pulse
motion.purpose: Accent (ink) release pulse when an agent earns autonomy. Never an alarm color.

motion.name: ret-pulse
motion.purpose: The active-incident banner dot. Reduced-motion → static.

motion.name: ret-health-ring
motion.purpose: One-shot expanding ring (in the health color) on a fleet status transition / auto-demotion.

motion.name: ret-bracket-dissolve + ret-shatter
motion.purpose: The REMOVE OVERSIGHT signature beat: the guardrail bracket dissolves and the last chain link shatters as an agent goes fully autonomous.

motion.name: ret-tool-flash
motion.purpose: An active capability in the tool rail flashes gently.

motion.name: sonifier
motion.value: 55Hz drone (gain tracks aggregate health) + panned earcons — rising [392,523,659] recover/promote, falling [330,247] degrade/demote
motion.purpose: Opt-in ambient sound, off by default: health as a low drone, transitions as discrete panned earcons. Always a redundant cue, never the sole signal.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Non-negotiable: every ceremony degrades to an instant state change + ledger/aria-live announcement.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The committing action (Approve / Promote). Solid ink fill, square, mono.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action (Deny / Escalate). Transparent, hairline border, dim mono label.

components.name: Filter Chips
components.description: The Causal Search / view filter chips. Mono uppercase, square; active = ink fill + aria-pressed.

components.name: Lens Tab Strip
components.description: The view-mode tab strip over the shared store. One hairline-bordered strip; active tab = ink fill + aria-selected.

components.name: Search Input
components.description: The Causal Search input. Hairline container, mono text, ink focus ring; leading search glyph.

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: The color=health pairing: a health dot is ALWAYS accompanied by a text label — status is never color-only.

components.name: Severity Badge
components.description: Incident severity marker. Square, hairline, health-colored by severity; the color carries the alarm, the text carries the level.

narrative.overview: Reticle is a drafting-table blueprint you operate from a calm control room. The world is a 48px dot-grid plane; agents, services, incidents and the production estate are plotted on it as instruments, not cards. Structure is drawn with 1px hairlines and square corners; nothing is rounded, nothing is shadowed, nothing decorates. The surface stays deliberately quiet — a near-black field under mono readouts — so that the one thing allowed to be loud, agent health, is the only saturated color on screen. It is dual-theme (a true light blueprint exists), but dark is its face: a control room at 3am. Motion is reserved — a heartbeat on a degrading agent, a sonar ping on a critical one, a release pulse when an agent earns autonomy — never ambient decoration, always reduced-motion-gated. Its sensory layer follows Amelia Wattenberger's thesis ('Our interfaces have lost their senses', 'Evolving the infinite canvas'): health reaches the operator through peripheral warmth, motion, sound, and spatial memory — each a redundant cue, never the sole signal.
keyCharacteristics: Square corners (--ret-card-radius: 0), 1px hairlines, zero shadows — flat by construction.
Saturated color = agent health, and nothing else; the accent is an ink, never a hue.
Mono data everywhere: numbers are tabular Geist Mono; labels are mono uppercase tracked.
A 48px dot-grid world with drafting guides (rail/cross) as the spatial substrate.
Calm by default; motion signals real state change and is always reduced-motion-gated.
Ambient senses (after Wattenberger): temperature, breathing/sonar motion, and an opt-in sonifier as redundant health cues — never the sole signal.

rules.name: The One-Color-One-Meaning Rule
rules.body: Saturated color means agent health — full stop. It is always paired with a text label + a shape/position cue; status is never conveyed by color alone.

rules.name: The Ink-Accent Rule
rules.body: --ret-accent is an ink (near-white #ededed dark / near-black #18181b light), not a hue. It carries active / primary / autonomy and must never read as a fifth status color.

rules.name: The Ink-Not-Hue Autonomy Rule
rules.body: Autonomy is encoded by left→right position + a monochrome ink fill bar + a chain/shackle icon + a mono label — never by color.

rules.name: The Mono-Data Rule
rules.body: Every number is Geist Mono with tabular-nums so live-ticking columns never reflow; labels and units are mono uppercase tracked and muted so the value reads first.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest and stay flat. If you reach for a box-shadow to separate two things, use a 1px hairline or a tonal step instead. Square corners only (lone exception: the critical card's state-lift).

rules.name: The Senses Rule
rules.body: Health reaches the operator through redundant ambient channels — temperature, motion (a breathing/pinging status dot), and an opt-in sonifier (drone + panned earcons) — each layered on the text label, never replacing it. Every channel obeys prefers-reduced-motion; sound is off until asked.

dos: Do keep saturated color for agent health only (#4ade80 / #fbbf24 / #f87171), always paired with a text label and a shape/position cue.
Do use --ret-accent as an ink for the active control, the primary action, and the autonomy fill — never as a status color.
Do set every number in Geist Mono with tabular-nums; set labels in mono uppercase tracked and muted.
Do separate regions with 1px hairlines and the tonal ladder (bg → surface → surface-hover); keep corners square.
Do gate every animation behind prefers-reduced-motion, and give canvas surfaces a keyboard + screen-reader path (the List lens is the spine).
Do treat motion, temperature, and sound as redundant ambient cues for health (Wattenberger's senses) — reserved for genuine state change and layered on the text label.
donts: Don't build the generic SaaS dashboard: no endless identical card grids, no hero-metric template, no cool-gray + single-accent monoculture (Datadog/Grafana sameness).
Don't drift into the dark hacker cliché — no neon-on-black, no matrix-rain, no terminal-as-costume.
Don't go consumer/playful (rounded friendly shapes, bright multi-color, mascots) or heavy-enterprise (dense gray tables, Bootstrap/AdminLTE chrome, modal-on-modal).
Don't use border-left/border-right > 1px as a colored side-stripe; use full borders, tints, or leading marks.
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or any box-shadow for elevation.
Don't convey status by color alone, and don't let the accent or signal-blue read as a fifth health state.
Don't round corners or introduce a second accent hue; square + ink is the system.
Don't autoplay sound, animate for decoration, or let any single channel (color, motion, or sound) be the only carrier of a signal.
